name: whosthere
description: Local Area Network discovery tool with an interactive Terminal User Interface (TUI) written in Go.  Discover, explore, and understand your LAN in an intuitive way. Knock Knock.. who's there? 🚪
readme_summary: Whosthere is a Go-based Local Area Network discovery tool that uses an interactive Terminal User Interface (TUI) to help users understand their network. It employs multiple unprivileged scanning techniques like mDNS, SSDP, and ARP cache analysis, enriching discovered devices with manufacturer information (OUI lookups).
primary_use_case: Discovering and visualizing devices on a local area network through an interactive terminal interface.
cover_image_prompt: A friendly, curious robot holding a magnifying glass, peering into a stylized, glowing network cloud. The robot is pointing its magnifying glass at various small, colorful icons representing devices (laptops, phones, smart TVs) within the cloud. Subtle data streams flow from the devices to the robot, and a small, minimalist UI element showing device names and IPs is visible on the robot's chest. The background is a soft, dark blue with faint circuit board patterns. The overall mood is helpful and informative, like a digital detective. The art style should be a clean, 3D isometric illustration with a bright, inviting color palette.

name: homehub
description: A private, lightweight, no-login, self-hosted family utility for your home and it comes with no config PWA.
readme_summary: HomeHub is a private, self-hosted, and lightweight web application designed to centralize family utilities. It offers a no-login, Progressive Web App (PWA) experience for managing shared notes, shopping lists, chores, media downloads, and more, all within your home network.
key_features: Customizable features and theme via config.yml
primary_use_case: To provide a centralized, private, and easy-to-use dashboard for families to manage various daily utilities and information on their home network.
cover_image_prompt: A cozy, modern home dashboard depicted as a central hub with various interconnected modules. Imagine a stylized, glowing orb at the center, radiating gentle light. From this orb, streams of data flow into distinct, intuitive icons representing family activities: a shopping cart for lists, a calendar for events, a notepad for notes, a cloud for shared files, and a video play button for media downloads. Subtle digital particles and clean UI elements are visible. The overall scene should feel organized, private, and family-friendly, perhaps with a soft, warm color palette. The style should be a 3D isometric illustration, emphasizing clarity and accessibility.

name: cyberpanel
description: Cyber Panel - The hosting control panel for OpenLiteSpeed
readme_summary: CyberPanel is a web hosting control panel powered by OpenLiteSpeed, designed to simplify server and website management. It offers a wide range of features for managing domains, emails, databases, security, and PHP versions, making it a comprehensive solution for hosting providers and developers.
primary_use_case: Web hosting management and server administration.
cover_image_prompt: A friendly, futuristic control tower overseeing a bustling digital city. The tower's main console displays a simplified dashboard with icons for websites, emails, and security. From the tower, beams of light connect to various buildings, representing seamless management of domains, databases, and SSL certificates. Small, glowing data streams flow between the tower and the city, symbolizing efficient server operations. The overall scene is clean, bright, and conveys a sense of order and control, with the 'CyberPanel' logo subtly integrated into the tower's design. The style should be a 3D isometric illustration with vibrant colors and clear, intuitive iconography.

name: openlitespeed
description: Our high-performance, lightweight, open source HTTP server
readme_summary: OpenLiteSpeed is a high-performance, lightweight, open-source HTTP server developed by LiteSpeed Technologies. It is distributed under the GPLv3 license and offers robust web serving capabilities with a focus on speed and efficiency.
primary_use_case: Serving web content efficiently and at high speeds.
cover_image_prompt: A majestic, swift eagle soaring through a digital sky, carrying a glowing package labeled 'Website Data' towards a modern city. The eagle's wings are made of clean, flowing code, and its eyes emit a bright, efficient light. Below, the city's buildings are interconnected by fast, glowing data streams. Subtle UI elements showing speed metrics and HTTP/3 symbols are integrated into the sky. The scene is vibrant and dynamic, rendered in a 3D isometric illustration style with a focus on speed and clarity, embodying the 'OpenLiteSpeed' name on a distant cloud.

name: nightingale
description: Nightingale is to monitoring and alerting what Grafana is to visualization.
readme_summary: Nightingale is an open-source alerting system designed to work with existing data sources, focusing on processing and distributing alarms. It complements visualization tools like Grafana by providing a robust alerting engine and supports distributed deployments for resilience.
key_features: Connects with various data sources (VictoriaMetrics, ElasticSearch, etc.)
AI assistant integration via MCP-Server for natural language interaction
Distributed deployment mode for edge data centers
primary_use_case: To provide a powerful and flexible alerting engine for monitoring systems, processing and distributing alarms effectively, and integrating with existing data sources and visualization tools.
cover_image_prompt: A vigilant lighthouse keeper on a stormy night, diligently tending to a powerful lighthouse beam that cuts through the darkness, illuminating distant ships. The lighthouse represents the Nightingale system, its beam symbolizing the alerting function that warns of potential dangers (represented by dark clouds and rough seas). Small, glowing data streams flow into the base of the lighthouse, feeding its operational power. In the background, faint outlines of ships represent monitored systems. The scene is set on a rocky outcrop with dramatic, moody lighting. The image should be in a digital painting style with clear outlines and a focus on the contrast between light and dark, conveying a sense of security and proactive warning. The name 'Nightingale' is subtly etched into the lighthouse base.
